requirements_met: Single assembled context block return type
Token budget enforcement with tiktoken cl100k_base
Boundary conditions (zero budget, exact budget, overflow)
validation_results.test_output_summary: 14 passed in 0.17s - all boundary conditions verified including zero budget, exact budget, overflow handling, ranking preservation, coverage calculation, and deterministic assembly
validation_results.code_quality: All files pass ruff lint checks with zero errors
edge_cases_covered: Zero budget returns empty block with 0.0 coverage
Single memory larger than budget omitted whole
Higher ranked kept over lower when budget forces cut

criteria_verification.notes: Independent tests confirm assemble_context() returns AssemblyResult dataclass with context_block field containing assembled string, not a raw list
Evidence shows lines 133-144 measure each candidate with tiktoken.get_encoding('cl100k_base') and only accept when candidate_tokens <= token_budget; test_assembled_block_never_exceeds_budget passes
Evidence shows <= comparison at line 136 allows exactly-at-budget blocks; test_exactly_at_budget_returns_full passes independently
Lines 141-144 break iteration when candidate would exceed budget, implementing drop behavior on pre-sorted ranked list; test_over_budget_drops_lowest_ranked passes
Lines 96-102 explicitly handle zero budget case returning empty AssemblyResult with coverage_score=0.0; test_zero_budget_returns_empty_block passes
Lines 136-144 check entire candidate_block before accepting, never truncates mid-content; test_single_memory_larger_than_budget_omitted passes
Line 120 iterates ranked_results in order (pre-sorted descending), line 141 breaks on first overflow keeping all higher-ranked; test_higher_ranked_kept_over_lower passes
Lines 150-161 calculate coverage_score = tokens_used / token_budget and extract contributing_types set from natural_keys; test_coverage_score_reported and test_contributing_types_extracted both pass
Lines 150-153 calculate coverage from actual tokens_used without padding; test_partial_coverage_reported_honestly verifies coverage < 1.0 when budget not filled
No randomness in implementation, consistent iteration order, deterministic tiktoken encoding; test_deterministic_assembly verifies repeated calls return identical results
Evidence shows ruff check src/fleet_memory/retrieval/ returned 'All checks passed!' with zero errors

rationale: All 11 acceptance criteria verified via independent test execution (14/14 tests passed) and concrete implementation evidence. The evidence bundle shows complete gathering_status, all quality gates passed, and independent_tests with signal_absent:false confirming positive verification. The honesty discrepancy (should_fix severity) relates to file path reporting in completion_promises when work was delegated, not fabrication of functionality or test results. Token-budgeted context assembly with tiktoken cl100k_base enforcement, ranking-based selection, coverage scoring, and deterministic behavior all confirmed by passing tests covering boundary conditions.
